hazard.summary: An internally-generated credential cooldown message contained rate-limit language that matched the retryable error classifier, causing the retry handler to re-enter on its own synthesized recovery-path error. A single upstream 429 could produce cascading empty assistant error entries and break session resume.
signals: internally-generated credential cooldown message matched isRetryableError regex
single Anthropic 429 produced 3-4 cascading empty assistant error entries
session resume corrupted after repeated empty error entries
PR #3548 removed or reworded internal cooldown error so it no longer matched retryable patterns

constraints: Internally-generated cooldown or recovery messages must never match external retryable error classifiers.
Retry handlers must not re-enter on their own synthesized error text.
Recovery-path wording must be lexically disjoint from retry-trigger patterns.
A single upstream 429 must not fan out into repeated internal error entries.
verification: Trigger a provider 429 that enters the internal cooldown path.
Verify the cooldown message no longer matches retryable error patterns.
Verify the retry handler does not re-enter on synthesized cooldown errors.
Verify only one user-visible error is emitted for the incident.
Verify session resume remains intact with no cascading empty assistant entries.
notes: This is distinct from PT-2026-04-02-github-gsd-3430-billing-gate-message-only-stop. The earlier receipt captured message-only STOP classification and wrong enforcement scope. This receipt captures a second-order recovery-path failure: the system's own cooldown/recovery text was classified as retryable, causing recursive retry handling and cascading empty session errors. The merged fix in PR #3548 explicitly removed or reworded the internal cooldown message path so it would not match the retry classifier.

constraints: internal recovery text must not trigger retry classification
retry handlers must not re-enter on synthesized errors
single 429 must not create cascading internal error entries
anti_patterns: using the same lexical markers for external rate limits and internal cooldown messages
